reasoning: Both audits confirm PKGBUILD uses trusted sources (dl.google.com) with checksum verification and contains only standard installation logic. No evidence of malicious execution during makepkg.
coverage_issues: qwen model reviewed only PKGBUILD/package.xml despite 5010 files. Critical gaps: no review of install hooks, build scripts, or source files with network/filesystem access.
deepseek model wasted resources reviewing 10 animation XMLs (low-risk) while higher-risk files may exist.
false_positives: deepseek's animation XML reviews: Overreacted to static UI assets (normal in Android packages) not executed during makepkg.
learnings: File selection: Prioritize build/install scripts over static assets. Models should focus on PKGBUILD-referenced files and executables.
Prompt improvement: Explicitly instruct models to ignore development/config files unless referenced in PKGBUILD functions.
Failure mode: Models confuse 'thoroughness' with reviewing irrelevant files. Need better risk-based file prioritization heuristics.
